session_note: S55 — autonomous hardening batch pre-approved by user ('work top recommended priority batch autonomously i pre-approve your full batch and work. be thorough pedantic methodical creative technical coherent and logical'). Standard-tier 3-priority batch hardening the S52-S54 netcode (zero forward-design dependencies; mechanic-design + 2-peer smoke deferred to a supervised session). P1 (#5) — Sym F E2E flake fix (test-only): smoke.spec.ts Sym F fires dragSparkTo 3x with no spark-availability-wait + no null-check on the 2nd/3rd; spark-starvation race -> <3 prims -> '>=3' wait times out -> flake (recurred S53+S54). FIX: new helper placeFreeSparkAndConfirm(page,x,y) = wait-for-in-zone-Free-spark -> drag (1 bounded re-drag on null per Council #4) -> assert not-null -> wait-for-primitive-count-increment. P2 (#3) — protocol-mismatch FULL 2-bundle E2E: validate the S54-activated HELLO->mismatch->UX+drop-latch system over a real cross-browser wire (S54 PRIME-AUDIT flagged it as zero-observable-runtime-coverage). Send-side-only seam readTestProtoVersionOverride() (window-guarded, mirrors 3 existing __TEST_*__ seams); buildHello stamps override in a TEST-ONLY branch (cast quarantined; production path stays protoVersion:PROTOCOL_VERSION, preserving the bump-lockstep tsc tripwire per Council #1 SYNTHESIS). joiner override=2 -> host detects 'other older'; +directional case override=4 -> host detects 'you older' (Council #2). Add lobbyScreen.getStatusText() DEV accessor (Council #3 — proves the UX chain, not incidental). P3 (#4) — controls.test.ts greenfield foundation: vitest node-env CANNOT instantiate Controls (constructor binds DOM/window) -> extract behavior-preserving PURE predicates (codebase S10 #test-via-pure-helper-export pattern): decideKeyShrink (Q-key guard), computeReleaseGates (LMB-up isClient-branching gates incl S45 client-bypass), export distToSegment+computeStiffnessTier, test stepAttractLerp. A.0 STATE-DISCOVERY (verified this session via Read/Grep): vitest=node (no jsdom/happy-dom installed; vite.config test has no environment key); 3 existing __TEST_*__ seams at constants.ts:86/186/358; Sym F flake = smoke.spec.ts:281-282 unguarded drags; __SPARK__ exposes netTransport.getDiagnostics()+lobbyScreen; no read accessor for statusText; no existing controls.test.ts; HELLO emitted by both peers via wireHelloOnJoin (host hostHandlers.ts:147 verified; joiner clientHandlers.ts to verify in P2 A.0). 3-way Council R1 (Grok DISRUPTOR temp 0.4 + Gemini AUDITOR temp 0.5).
council_battle_ledger: 6-row ledger (Standard R1). (1) #3 `as 3` cast vs relax HelloMsg.protoVersion to number — Grok HIGH 'type lie' + Gemini Dec-1 -> SYNTHESIS: quarantine the cast to a TEST-ONLY branch in buildHello; production path stays `protoVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION` (clean, no cast) which PRESERVES the bump-lockstep tsc tripwire (bumping PROTOCOL_VERSION without updating HelloMsg.protoVersion still errors). Better than both inline-cast and type-relax (relax would weaken the contract for parseNetMessage/detectProtocolMismatch readers). (2) #3 'only host detects -> not FULL 2-bundle' — Grok HIGH -> SYNTHESIS(partial): detection IS correctly asymmetric (current peer detects stale peer = the real scenario); ADD a 2nd directional case (override=4 -> host sees newer peer -> 'Your version is older' branch); rename test to not overclaim symmetry. OVERRULE 'joiner must also detect' (stale peer runs old code by definition; forcing current-build-as-joiner is the same code path already covered). (3) #3 getStatusText vs diagnostics-only — Grok 'incidental' -> OVERRULE: getStatusText is the CORE deliverable (proves the onProtocolMismatch->format->onLobbyError->setErrorMessage UX chain the S54 PRIME-AUDIT flagged unverified); Grok's cited 'peerDropped event' DOES NOT EXIST (latch is a private Set, no emit) = hallucination. Keep layered: rejected>=1 (detection) + getStatusText contains 'Protocol mismatch'+'v2'+'v3'+'older' (UX). (4) #5 robust-helper TOCTOU — Grok MED + alt state-injection/DOM-locator -> SYNTHESIS: keep helper (tests real flow), add ONE bounded re-drag to close the tiny single-actor TOCTOU, failure is loud-throw not silent-flake. OVERRULE page.locator('.prim') (prims are Pixi canvas, NO DOM nodes = hallucination) + state-injection (bypasses the placement flow Sym F exists to test). (5) order+tier — Grok 'P2 before P1' -> OVERRULE: P1/P2 touch unrelated test areas; P1's new helper is a DEPENDENCY of P2 (must precede). Keep P1->P2->P3, Standard 1-round. (6) seam hygiene (bundle/security) — Grok ADD LOW + Gemini Ch-2 -> ADOPT doc comment: seam ships in bundle but harmless (window-guarded -> prod default); worst-case abuse = SELF-latch (no cross-peer attack), identical safety to the 3 shipped seams. REJECTED (premature/over-eng): memoize seam read, curry isClient, branded Version<3> type, getTestSeam() DRY helper (inconsistent w/ 3 existing per-seam readers + cross-file scope), skip distToSegment/computeStiffnessTier (cheap, high-value, currently untested). Gemini APPROVE (soft 'missing' all out-of-scope/addressed: P2 recovery-UX = refresh-is-the-UX by design; E2E runtime ~+30-60s acceptable; E2E coverage is behavioral-assertion-based).
prime_audit: PRIME-AUDIT delta (Prime Architect — 3 execution guardrails, no design change). (1) Runtime-Verifiability (Rule 20 boot-then-smoke): Council + I both ASSUMED the joiner wires HELLO via the override-able buildHello; I verified the HOST side (hostHandlers.ts:147) but only INFERRED the joiner. MUST Read clientHandlers.ts before P2 to confirm the joiner calls wireHelloOnJoin — else the joiner never emits its v2 HELLO, the host never detects, and the E2E silently never fires (false-green). Logged as P2 A.0. (2) P2 E2E must WAIT not assert-after-connect: the mismatch fires asynchronously AFTER peerCount>=1 when the HELLO arrives; the test must waitForWorld-poll diagnostics.rejected>=1 (with timeout) BEFORE asserting status text (a synchronous post-connect assert would race the in-flight HELLO). (3) P3 extraction must capture the genuine isClient-branching (S45 client-bypass: reachable=isClient?true:reachCheck; inZone=isClient?false:zoneCheck; inTerritory=isClient?false:territoryCheck) — NOT collapse to a trivial `reachable && !inZone && !inTerritory` AND that loses the bypass logic. Plus bundle watch: 1.13 KB headroom under the 500 KB charter is thin; verify `npm run build` < 500 KB after P2 (seam + accessor ship in bundle; P1/P3 are bundle-neutral/test-only). Grok-CHECK fact-check rule REINFORCED: R1 produced 2 hallucinations already (non-existent peerDropped event; non-existent .prim DOM locator) — 4th consecutive Grok phase with fabricated code references; verify every cited file:line via Read/Grep before adopting any CHECK BLOCKER/HIGH.

priorities.label: S55 P1 (#5) — Sym F E2E flake fix (spark-starvation race in smoke.spec.ts territorial-block test)
priorities.check_method: P1 verified: Sym F E2E (smoke.spec.ts:257 'Host placement inside joiner territory is silently rejected') passed 3/3 consecutive local runs (--repeat-each=3, retries=0, 21.4s/20.9s/20.5s consistent timing) — the spark-starvation flake that produced fail-then-pass-on-retry in S53+S54 (Playwright '1 flaky') is eliminated. Root cause fixed: the 2nd/3rd of three back-to-back dragSparkTo calls had no spark-availability wait and no null-check; dragSparkTo returns null when no Free spark is in the 200px pick-zone at call time, so a starvation moment placed <3 prims and the '>=3 blue prims' wait timed out. New e2e/helpers.ts placeFreeSparkAndConfirm(page,x,y) makes each placement deterministic: wait-for-in-zone-Free-spark -> drag -> bounded single re-drag on null (Council #4 SYNTHESIS) -> wait-for-primitive-count-increment (host-authoritative round-trip); throws loud on a genuine no-spark/non-landing condition instead of silent flake. RALPH:PATROL hypotheses all clear: (a) ZERO production/bundle impact — only e2e/ touched, which vite.config.ts:31 excludes from build AND vitest excludes from collection; (b) full unit suite 842/842 GREEN unchanged (e2e not collected), confirming no src/ regression; (c) tsc -b --noEmit clean (e2e is in typecheck scope — new helper + import compile); (d) the '>=3 blue prims' backstop + downstream territory hard-block assertion retained, so coverage is strictly increased, not weakened; (e) helper's count-based landing check is sound for the single-actor (joiner-only) Sym F setup — each PLACE_FROM_FREE increments world.primitives by exactly 1. Commit fb11fc0 pushed origin/master (a83ff13..fb11fc0); CI E2E (2-browser) + Deploy triggered.

priorities.label: S55 P2 (#3) — protocol-mismatch FULL 2-bundle E2E (send-side __TEST_PROTO_VERSION_OVERRIDE__ seam + getStatusText accessor)
priorities.check_method: P2 verified: 2 NEW cross-browser protocol-mismatch E2E tests PASS (smoke.spec.ts 'Protocol mismatch' describe — v2 'other player older' 6.0s + v4 'your version older' 5.0s). Each asserts the FULL S54 HELLO->mismatch chain over a real wire: (a) diagnostics.rejected>=1 (host detected + dropped the stale-peer HELLO — the per-peer latch fired); (b) lobbyScreen status text contains 'Protocol mismatch' + peer version ('v2'/'v4') + local 'v3' + the correct direction-aware advice ('older' / 'your version is older') — proving onProtocolMismatch->formatProtocolMismatchMessage->onLobbyError->setErrorMessage end-to-end; (c) joiner status stays clean (the send-side override is context-isolated — joiner's PROTOCOL_VERSION=3 sees host's HELLO(3) as a match). Production change (DEV/test-guarded): protocol.ts readTestProtoVersionOverride() send-side seam (mirrors the 3 existing constants.ts __TEST_*__ seams; window-guarded; prod stamps the local PROTOCOL_VERSION) + buildHello applies the override in a TEST-ONLY branch with the `as 3` cast QUARANTINED there (production path keeps `protoVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION` and the version-bump lockstep tsc tripwire — Council #1); lobbyScreen.getStatusText() DEV accessor (Council #3) + an error-sticky errorLatched latch. RALPH:PATROL + PRIME-AUDIT Runtime-Verifiability CAUGHT A REAL BUG that static review + Council + tsc + 844 unit all passed green: the FIRST E2E run FAILED (host showed 'Player 2 connected!' not the mismatch text) because the errorLatched latch was HALF-WIRED — declared (field) + checked (updatePeerStatus guard) + cleared (reset) but setErrorMessage never SET it (1-of-4 edits missed). A mismatch HELLO arriving before the first post-connect frame was thus clobbered by updatePeerStatus, permanently hiding the refresh-prompt UX — a latent PRODUCTION bug, not merely a test artifact. Completing the latch (setErrorMessage sets errorLatched=true) fixed both production UX + the test; re-run PASSED 6.0s/5.0s. Regression: baseline lobby->play (6.1s) + Sym F (19.6s) PASS unchanged (errorLatched stays false in non-error flows so updatePeerStatus behaves as before; the seam is a prod no-op without the window override). Unit 842->844 GREEN (+2 seam tests: numeric override applied; NaN/string/absent fall through to PROTOCOL_VERSION). tsc -b clean. Bundle 498.87->499.21 KB (+0.34 KB; 0.79 KB headroom under the 500 KB charter — THIN, flagged for handoff anti-bloat carry). Commit 975ba5a pushed origin/master; CI E2E + Deploy triggered.

priorities.label: S55 P3 (#4) — controls.test.ts foundation suite via behavior-preserving pure-helper extraction
priorities.check_method: P3 verified: NEW src/input/controls.test.ts foundation suite, 27 tests all GREEN; full suite 844->871. tsc -b clean. Extraction is behavior-preserving (vitest is node-env — Controls can't be instantiated since its constructor binds canvas/window listeners; followed the S10 #test-via-pure-helper-export pattern): decideKeyShrink (Q-key SHRINK_TERRITORY guard, 5 conditions) + computeReleaseGates (LMB-up gating capturing the S45/S49 isClient client-bypass — deliberately NOT a trivial AND, per PRIME-AUDIT #3) extracted from onKeyDown/onUp; distToSegment + computeStiffnessTier exported; stepAttractLerp tested. Coverage: decideKeyShrink 9 (q/Q accept; non-Q reject; INPUT/TEXTAREA focus block; BODY/CANVAS/undefined-focus pass; solo reject; LOBBY/WIN/TITLE/POSTGAME reject; 0-charge reject; 1/2-charge pass; absent-player reject); computeReleaseGates 7 (host: commit-all-pass / reach-fail / exactly-at-max-reach / in-zone / in-territory; client: distance-bypass / zone+territory-ignored); distToSegment 6 (on-segment / both endpoints / perpendicular / clamp-before-A / clamp-past-B / degenerate zero-length); computeStiffnessTier 2 (anchor->MID + 36-combo delegation matches lookupCombo); stepAttractLerp 3 (rate 0.5 lerp + prevPos=old / rate-0 no-op / rate-1 reaches cursor). RALPH:PATROL — behavior-preservation confirmed EMPIRICALLY by E2E (not assumed): baseline lobby->play (5.9s) + Sym A joiner LMB-place (10.4s, exercises computeReleaseGates isClient=true client-bypass in the LIVE onUp) + Sym F host territory hard-block (18.8s, exercises the host reach/zone/territory gates in the LIVE onUp) ALL PASS, proving the refactored handlers behave identically; onKeyDown guards unchanged (same 5 conditions, same order, same dispatch). Bundle 499.21->499.61 KB (+0.40 KB from the 2 extracted exported fns + param-object call sites; 0.39 KB headroom under the 500 KB charter — THIN; anti-bloat carry now URGENT, main.ts 888-LOC trim is the prime candidate). Commit 072ec44 pushed origin/master; CI E2E + Deploy triggered.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S55 #sym-f-flake-was-unguarded-spark-drag-not-network — the recurring Sym F E2E flake (S53+S54 fail-then-pass-on-retry) was NOT a WebRTC/relay issue but a test-setup spark-starvation race: three back-to-back dragSparkTo calls with no availability-wait + no null-check. dragSparkTo silently returns null when no Free spark sits in the 200px pick-zone at call time -> fewer than 3 prims -> the downstream '>=3 blue prims' wait timed out. Fix: placeFreeSparkAndConfirm makes each placement deterministic (wait-for-in-zone-Free-spark -> drag -> bounded single re-drag on null -> wait-for-primitive-count-increment). Lesson: a helper that can silently no-op (return null) is a flake generator when called without checking its result — confirm the OBSERVABLE EFFECT (the primitive landed), not merely that the action was attempted. 3/3 local --repeat-each=3 (retries=0) confirmed the fix.

S55 #half-wired-latch-caught-by-e2e-not-static-review — the errorLatched UX-stickiness latch added for P2 was declared (field) + checked (updatePeerStatus guard) + cleared (reset) but setErrorMessage never SET it (a 1-of-4-edits miss). tsc -b + 844 unit tests + the 3-way Council ALL passed it green; only the cross-browser E2E caught it (host showed 'Player 2 connected!' instead of the mismatch text). Lesson: a multi-point state mechanism (declare/set/check/clear) must have ALL points verified together — a missing SET is invisible to type-checking and to unit tests that don't exercise the live integration. Runtime-Verifiability (boot-then-smoke) is the ONLY pass that catches a half-wired guard, which is exactly why PRIME-AUDIT mandates it for runtime-infra/UX PDRs. The miss was also a latent PRODUCTION UX defect (mismatch refresh-prompt clobbered by routine peer-status), not merely a test artifact.

S55 #send-side-protoversion-override-quarantined-cast — to E2E-test the protocol-mismatch system over a real wire, the cleanest seam is a SEND-side-only window.__TEST_PROTO_VERSION_OVERRIDE__ (one peer announces a stale version) rather than relaxing HelloMsg.protoVersion to `number` (Council #1). Quarantining the `as 3` cast to buildHello's test-only branch keeps the production return path `protoVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION`, which preserves the version-bump lockstep tsc tripwire (raising PROTOCOL_VERSION without updating HelloMsg.protoVersion still errors). Send-side-only also faithfully models real deploy-skew: the current-version peer detects the stale one — detection is inherently asymmetric, so 'host-detects-stale-joiner' is the real scenario, not a test shortcut.

S55 #node-env-forces-pure-helper-extraction-for-input-tests — vitest runs in node (no jsdom/happy-dom installed; vite.config `test` has no `environment` key), so the Controls class (its constructor binds canvas + window event listeners) cannot be instantiated nor driven with synthetic DOM events. The codebase-idiomatic answer (S10 #test-via-pure-helper-export — already used by stepAttractLerp / pickRedundantBondTargets / lerpColor / sanitizeRoomCodeValue) is to extract the DECISION logic into pure functions and unit-test those, with the e2e Sym specs as the live-handler integration backstop. computeReleaseGates deliberately retains the isClient branching (NOT a trivial reachable&&!inZone&&!inTerritory AND) so the S45/S49 client-bypass — the load-bearing asymmetry — is the thing under test. Behavior-preservation was CONFIRMED empirically by re-running Sym A (client place) + Sym F (host territory) GREEN, never assumed.

S55 #check-triumvirate-ship-grok-no-hallucination-at-temp-0.3 — CHECK Triumvirate verdict SHIP (RALPH:PATROL PASS inline per-priority + GROK-ANALYST PASS + GEMINI-AUDITOR PASS 5/5/5). Notably Grok produced ZERO hallucinations this CHECK (breaking the 4-phase S53/S54 streak), citing only real code — likely from temp 0.3 + an explicit 'needs-verification, do not guess line numbers' system framing. Its one MED (add a NODE_ENV/production guard to readTestProtoVersionOverride) was fact-checked VALID-but-low-value and NOT adopted: the abuse is benign SELF-DoS (Council #6 already adjudicated; identical to the 3 shipped __TEST_*__ seams) and guarding only 1 of 4 seams would be inconsistent. Calibration takeaway: low temp + verification-framing materially reduces Grok CHECK false-positives — worth standardizing in the CHECK prompt.

S55 #eos-audit-caught-ci-billing-block — END-OF-SESSION AUDIT (Rule 22) caught that the P3 push CI (E2E run 26661074955 + Deploy) FAILED in 4s — NOT a code failure but a GitHub ACCOUNT BILLING block ('recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased; the job was not started'). P1+P2 CI ran GREEN (E2E success 2m59s/3m24s + Deploy success); the limit was hit between 20:30 (P2) and 20:40 (P3), likely cumulative Actions minutes. Live site = 975ba5a (P1+P2 deployed); P3 (072ec44) verified locally (871 unit/tsc/e2e) but NOT deployed — zero user impact since P3's controls.ts change is behavior-preserving. USER ACTION REQUIRED: resolve GitHub 'Billing & plans'; until then ALL pushes' CI + Deploy are blocked (a re-run won't help). Lesson: the EOS audit's 'check the CI feed' step is load-bearing — local-green != shipped-green when the runner won't even start.

SESSION #s55-shipped-3-priorities-3-commits-871-green-bundle-499.61KB — fb11fc0 (P1 Sym F flake fix, e2e-only) + 975ba5a (P2 protocol-mismatch 2-bundle E2E + send-side seam + error-sticky latch) + 072ec44 (P3 controls.test.ts foundation, +27). Unit 842->871 (+29: +2 seam, +27 controls). tsc clean. Bundle 498.87->499.61 KB (+0.74 KB net; 0.39 KB headroom — anti-bloat carry now URGENT). Standard 3-way Council R1 (6-row ledger) + CHECK Triumvirate SHIP. PRIME-AUDIT Runtime-Verifiability caught a half-wired latch (P2) BEFORE ship; EOS audit caught a CI billing block. API ~$0.05 (Grok 2, Gemini 2). Context 39% GREEN at close. Net effect: S52-S54 netcode hardened — Sym F flake eliminated (3/3), the S54-activated protocol-mismatch system now has real cross-browser runtime coverage + a UX-stickiness fix, and the controls input layer has a 27-test foundation.
